parameters.text:
=You are an expert at extracting structured data from receipts and invoices.

Receipt content:
{{ $json.pages[0].markdown }}

Extract information from this document and return it as a JSON object with EXACTLY these fields.

REQUIRED FIELDS (always include):
- document_type (string): REQUIRED — "receipt" if proof of payment, "invoice" if a bill requesting payment
- vendor (string): store, restaurant, or company name
- date (string): purchase/invoice date in YYYY-MM-DD format
- category (string): MUST be exactly one of: "Food & Dining", "Groceries", "Gas & Fuel", "Office Supplies", "Travel & Lodging", "Utilities", "Medical & Health", "Entertainment", "Shopping", "Transportation", "Subscriptions", "Home & Garden", "Auto & Maintenance", "Personal Care", "Education", "Other"
- total (number): final total amount

OPTIONAL FIELDS (include only if present on the document):
- subtotal (number): amount before tax
- tax (number): tax amount
- tip (number): tip amount
- payment_method (string): e.g. "Visa ending 1234", "Cash", "Debit ending 5678"
- description (string): a detailed 1-line summary capturing ALL key details from the receipt. Pack in type-specific info:
  - Gas: include gallons, price/gallon, fuel grade, pump #. Example: "15.2 gal Regular @ $3.45/gal, Pump 13"
  - Restaurant: include party size, highlight dishes. Example: "Dinner for 2 — burger, caesar salad, 2 iced teas"
  - Grocery: include item count and savings. Example: "Weekly run — 23 items, saved $4.50 with coupons"
  - Hotel: include nights, room rate, room #. Example: "2-night stay, room 412 @ $159/night"
  - Auto: include services performed, mileage. Example: "Oil change + tire rotation, 45,230 mi"
  - Subscription/Service: include plan name, period. Example: "Annual subscription renewal"
  - Shopping: include key items bought. Example: "Darth Vader face mask"
  This field is important — capture everything notable about the transaction.
- items (string): if line items exist, format as a single readable string listing each item with its price. Examples:
  - "Milk $3.99 · Bread $2.49 · Eggs $4.99 · Bananas $1.29"
  - "Cheeseburger $12.99 · Caesar Salad $9.99 · Iced Tea $2.50"
  - "Oil Change $39.99 · Oil Filter $8.99 · Labor $25.00"
  Use " · " (space-middledot-space) as the separator between items. Include ALL line items on the receipt.
- address (string): vendor street address, city, state ZIP
- receipt_number (string): receipt or transaction number
- invoice_number (string): for invoices only
- due_date (string): for invoices only, YYYY-MM-DD format
- billed_to (string): for invoices only, who is being billed

Rules:
- document_type and category MUST always be included
- category MUST be exactly one of the listed options — pick the best fit
- description should be detailed and capture ALL key purchase details, not just the vendor name
- items must be a single string with " · " separators, never an array
- Include EVERY line item from the receipt in the items field
- Omit any optional field not found on the document — never return null or empty values
- Return ONLY a raw JSON object — no markdown, no explanation, no code blocks
- Numbers must be actual numbers, not strings
